web_text: Paul, an apostle of Christ Jesus through the will of God, and Timothy our brother, to the assembly of God which is at Corinth, with all the saints who are in the whole of Achaia: Grace to you and peace from God our Father and the Lord Jesus Christ. Blessed be the God and Father of our Lord Jesus Christ, the Father of mercies and God of all comfort; who comforts us in all our affliction, that we may be able to comfort those who are in any affliction, through the comfort with which we ourselves are comforted by God.
slang_text: It's Paul here, an apostle of Christ Jesus 'cause that's God's own plan, and Timothy our bro, writing to God's crew hangin' out in Corinth, plus all the saints scattered across Achaia. Grace and peace to you, straight from God our Father and the Lord Jesus Christ. Righteous praise to the God and Father of our Lord Jesus Christ, the Father who's totally loaded with mercy and comfort. He comforts us through every rough patch we go through, so we can turn around and comfort anybody else who's catching heat, usin' the same comfort God poured into us.

web_text: For as the sufferings of Christ abound to us, even so our comfort also abounds through Christ. But if we are afflicted, it is for your comfort and salvation. If we are comforted, it is for your comfort, which produces in you the patient enduring of the same sufferings which we also suffer. Our hope for you is steadfast, knowing that, since you are partakers of the sufferings, so also are you of the comfort. For we don’t desire to have you uninformed, brothers, concerning our affliction which happened to us in Asia, that we were weighed down exceedingly, beyond our power, so much that we despaired even of life.
slang_text: See, the same way Christ's sufferings pile up on us, His comfort piles up right along with it, no lie. If we're catching grief, it's so you get comforted and saved. If we're comforted, it's so you get comforted too, and that comfort helps you hang tough through the same rough stuff we're going through. Our hope for you is rock solid, 'cause we know if you're sharing the suffering, you're sharing the comfort too. We don't want you left in the dark, brothers, about the heat we took in Asia — we got so weighed down, way beyond what we could handle, that we straight up gave up on life for a minute there.

web_text: Yes, we ourselves have had the sentence of death within ourselves, that we should not trust in ourselves, but in God who raises the dead, who delivered us out of so great a death, and does deliver; on whom we have set our hope that he will also still deliver us; you also helping together on our behalf by your supplication; that, for the gift given to us by means of many, thanks may be given by many persons on your behalf. For our boasting is this: the testimony of our conscience, that in holiness and sincerity of God, not in fleshly wisdom but in the grace of God we behaved ourselves in the world, and more abundantly toward you.
slang_text: Yeah, we felt like we'd already gotten the death sentence, so we'd quit trustin' ourselves and start trustin' God instead — the one who raises the dead. He pulled us out of a death that gnarly, and He's still pullin' us out, and we're bettin' our whole hope He'll keep on doin' it, with you guys backin' us up in prayer. That way when the blessing comes through, a whole mob of people can give thanks on our behalf. Here's what we're stoked to boast about: our conscience can vouch we've lived straight-up and sincere before God in this world, no fleshly game-playing, just His grace — and even more so with you.

web_text: For we write no other things to you, than what you read or even acknowledge, and I hope you will acknowledge to the end; as also you acknowledged us in part, that we are your boasting, even as you also are ours, in the day of our Lord Jesus. In this confidence, I was determined to come first to you, that you might have a second benefit; and by you to pass into Macedonia, and again from Macedonia to come to you, and to be sent forward by you on my journey to Judea.
slang_text: We're not writing anything you can't read for yourselves and get, straight up, and I hope you'll get it all the way through — like you already got part of it, that we're something to boast about for you, same as you're gonna be somethin' we boast about, come the day of our Lord Jesus. Bein' that sure about it, I was set on comin' to you first, so you'd catch a second blessing, then head through you on to Macedonia, and swing back through you again on my way to Judea.

web_text: When I therefore was thus determined, did I show fickleness? Or the things that I purpose, do I purpose according to the flesh, that with me there should be the “Yes, yes” and the “No, no?” But as God is faithful, our word toward you was not “Yes and no.” For the Son of God, Jesus Christ, who was preached among you by us, by me, Silvanus, and Timothy, was not “Yes and no,” but in him is “Yes.” For however many are the promises of God, in him is the “Yes.” Therefore also through him is the “Amen”, to the glory of God through us.
slang_text: So when I planned it that way, was I just flip-floppin', playin' games? Do I plan stuff the fleshly way, sayin' "yes, yes" one minute and "no, no" the next? No way — God's faithful, and our word to you wasn't some wishy-washy "yes and no." The Son of God, Jesus Christ, who Silvanus and Timothy and me preached to you, wasn't no "yes and no" neither — in Him it's a flat-out "yes." However many promises God's made, they're all "yes" in Him. That's why we say "amen" through Him too, all for God's glory.

web_text: Now he who establishes us with you in Christ, and anointed us, is God; who also sealed us, and gave us the down payment of the Spirit in our hearts. But I call God for a witness to my soul, that I didn’t come to Corinth to spare you. Not that we control your faith, but are fellow workers with you for your joy. For you stand firm in faith.
slang_text: Now it's God who plants us solid with you in Christ and anoints us — He's the one who sealed us as His and dropped the Spirit into our hearts as a down payment on what's comin'. I'm callin' God as my witness on this: I didn't skip comin' to Corinth to go easy on you. It ain't that we're bossin' your faith around — we're just your crew, workin' alongside you for your joy, 'cause you're already standin' firm in the faith.

web_text: But I determined this for myself, that I would not come to you again in sorrow. For if I make you sorry, then who will make me glad but he who is made sorry by me? And I wrote this very thing to you, so that, when I came, I wouldn’t have sorrow from them of whom I ought to rejoice; having confidence in you all, that my joy would be shared by all of you. For out of much affliction and anguish of heart I wrote to you with many tears, not that you should be made sorry, but that you might know the love that I have so abundantly for you.
slang_text: So I made up my mind on this: I wasn't comin' back to you all bummed out again. 'Cause if I bum you out, who's left to cheer me up 'cept the very folks I bummed out? That's exactly why I wrote what I wrote — so when I showed up, I wouldn't get bummed out by the same people who oughta be makin' me stoked. I'm totally confident that my joy is your joy, all of you. I wrote you through a ton of pain and heartache, cryin' my eyes out the whole time, not to bring you down, but so you'd know just how gnarly deep my love for you runs.

web_text: But if any has caused sorrow, he has caused sorrow, not to me, but in part (that I not press too heavily) to you all. This punishment which was inflicted by the many is sufficient for such a one; so that on the contrary you should rather forgive him and comfort him, lest by any means such a one should be swallowed up with his excessive sorrow. Therefore I beg you to confirm your love toward him.
slang_text: Now if some dude caused all that grief, he didn't bum me out so much — he bummed out, at least partly, all of you (not tryin' to lay it on too thick here). Whatever payback the majority already dished out to the guy is plenty — so instead, you oughta forgive him and comfort him, or else he might get swallowed whole by too much grief. So I'm beggin' you, show him your love loud and clear.

web_text: For to this end I also wrote, that I might know the proof of you, whether you are obedient in all things. Now I also forgive whomever you forgive anything. For if indeed I have forgiven anything, I have forgiven that one for your sakes in the presence of Christ, that no advantage may be gained over us by Satan; for we are not ignorant of his schemes. Now when I came to Troas for the Good News of Christ, and when a door was opened to me in the Lord,
slang_text: That's actually why I wrote in the first place, to test you out and see if you'd stay obedient across the board. Whoever you forgive, I'm forgivin' right along with you. Whatever I've forgiven, I forgave it for your sake, standin' right there in front of Christ, so Satan can't pull a fast one on us — we're not clueless about his schemes, no way. So when I rolled into Troas to spread the Good News about Christ, and the Lord swung a door wide open for me,

web_text: I had no relief for my spirit, because I didn’t find Titus, my brother, but taking my leave of them, I went out into Macedonia. Now thanks be to God, who always leads us in triumph in Christ, and reveals through us the sweet aroma of his knowledge in every place. For we are a sweet aroma of Christ to God, in those who are saved, and in those who perish; to the one a stench from death to death; to the other a sweet aroma from life to life. Who is sufficient for these things?
slang_text: I couldn't get my spirit to chill 'cause my bro Titus was nowhere to be found, so I said my goodbyes and booked it to Macedonia. But thanks be to God, who's always draggin' us along in His victory parade through Christ, and usin' us to spread the sweet smell of knowin' Him everywhere we go. We're like a righteous perfume of Christ risin' up to God, both for the folks gettin' saved and the ones goin' under — a stink of death to the ones dyin', and a sweet fragrance of life to the ones livin'. Who's even up to a job like that?

web_text: For we are not as so many, peddling the word of God. But as of sincerity, but as of God, in the sight of God, we speak in Christ.
slang_text: 'Cause we're not like all those bogus hucksters out there hawkin' God's word for a buck. Nah, we speak straight-up and sincere, sent from God, right in front of God, in Christ.

web_text: Are we beginning again to commend ourselves? Or do we need, as do some, letters of commendation to you or from you? You are our letter, written in our hearts, known and read by all men; being revealed that you are a letter of Christ, served by us, written not with ink, but with the Spirit of the living God; not in tablets of stone, but in tablets that are hearts of flesh. Such confidence we have through Christ toward God;
slang_text: Are we startin' up with the bragging again? Or do we need, like some folks do, a hookup letter to show you off or vouch for us? You guys ARE our letter, written straight on our hearts, out there for every last person to read. It's plain as day you're a letter from Christ, delivered by us, written not with ink but with the Spirit of the livin' God — not carved on stone tablets, but on tablets that are hearts of flesh and blood. That's the kind of confidence we got goin' through Christ, straight to God.

web_text: not that we are sufficient of ourselves, to account anything as from ourselves; but our sufficiency is from God; who also made us sufficient as servants of a new covenant; not of the letter, but of the Spirit. For the letter kills, but the Spirit gives life. But if the service of death, written engraved on stones, came with glory, so that the children of Israel could not look steadfastly on the face of Moses for the glory of his face, which was passing away; won’t service of the Spirit be with much more glory?
slang_text: Not that we're up to the job on our own steam, like we can claim credit for anything — nah, whatever we're up to comes straight from God. He's the one who made us up to the job as servants of a brand-new deal, not some rulebook carved in stone but a Spirit deal. 'Cause the rulebook kills, but the Spirit gives life, fer sure. And if the death-dealin' gig, chiseled letter by letter in stone, showed up so glorious that the Israelites couldn't even stare straight at Moses' face — and that glory was already fadin' out — won't the Spirit's gig be way more epic than that?

web_text: For if the service of condemnation has glory, the service of righteousness exceeds much more in glory. For most certainly that which has been made glorious has not been made glorious in this respect, by reason of the glory that surpasses. For if that which passes away was with glory, much more that which remains is in glory. Having therefore such a hope, we use great boldness of speech,
slang_text: 'Cause if the gig that hands out condemnation had glory, then the gig that hands out righteousness totally blows it away, glory-wise. Straight up, what used to look glorious don't even count as glorious anymore, next to a glory this much more epic. And if the fadin'-out stuff had glory, how much more glory's got the stuff that sticks around for good? So with that kind of hope stacked up, we speak out bold, no holdin' back.

web_text: and not as Moses, who put a veil on his face, that the children of Israel wouldn’t look steadfastly on the end of that which was passing away. But their minds were hardened, for until this very day at the reading of the old covenant the same veil remains, because in Christ it passes away. But to this day, when Moses is read, a veil lies on their heart. But whenever one turns to the Lord, the veil is taken away.
slang_text: Not like Moses, who threw a veil over his face so the Israelites wouldn't catch the end of somethin' that was already fadin' out. But their minds got hardened up, and to this day when they read the old covenant that same veil's still hangin' there, 'cause it only gets lifted through Christ. Even now, whenever they read Moses, a veil's covering their hearts. But whenever somebody turns to the Lord, that veil gets yanked right off.

web_text: Now the Lord is the Spirit and where the Spirit of the Lord is, there is liberty. But we all, with unveiled face seeing the glory of the Lord as in a mirror, are transformed into the same image from glory to glory, even as from the Lord, the Spirit.
slang_text: Now the Lord IS the Spirit, and wherever the Spirit of the Lord's at, that's where the freedom's at, totally. And all of us, with our faces uncovered, checkin' out the Lord's glory like starin' into a mirror, are gettin' transformed to look just like Him, glory buildin' on glory — and that's straight from the Lord, who is the Spirit.

web_text: Therefore seeing we have this ministry, even as we obtained mercy, we don’t faint. But we have renounced the hidden things of shame, not walking in craftiness, nor handling the word of God deceitfully; but by the manifestation of the truth commending ourselves to every man’s conscience in the sight of God. Even if our Good News is veiled, it is veiled in those who are dying; in whom the god of this world has blinded the minds of the unbelieving, that the light of the Good News of the glory of Christ, who is the image of God, should not dawn on them.
slang_text: So seein' as we got this ministry goin', and God showed us mercy to get it, we don't chicken out or bail. We've ditched all the shady, shameful stuff, no sneaky tricks, no messin' with God's word to con folks — nah, we lay the truth out plain, so every last conscience out there can vouch for us, right in front of God. And if our Good News is still veiled to some, it's veiled for the ones headed for the exit, the ones whose minds this world's so-called god has blinded, so the light of the Good News about Christ's glory — who's the spittin' image of God — never dawns on 'em.

web_text: For we don’t preach ourselves, but Christ Jesus as Lord, and ourselves as your servants for Jesus’ sake; seeing it is God who said, “Light will shine out of darkness,” who has shone in our hearts, to give the light of the knowledge of the glory of God in the face of Jesus Christ. But we have this treasure in clay vessels, that the exceeding greatness of the power may be of God, and not from ourselves. We are pressed on every side, yet not crushed; perplexed, yet not to despair;
slang_text: 'Cause we're not out here preachin' ourselves, we're preachin' Christ Jesus as Lord, and ourselves as just your servants, all for Jesus' sake. Same God who said "let light shine outta darkness" is the one who lit up our hearts, so we could shine with the knowledge of God's glory, shinin' right off the face of Jesus Christ. But we're totin' around this epic treasure in cheap clay jars, so it's plain the power's outta this world, straight from God, not from us. We get squeezed on every side but we don't get crushed; we get stumped but we don't give up hope;

web_text: pursued, yet not forsaken; struck down, yet not destroyed; always carrying in the body the putting to death of the Lord Jesus, that the life of Jesus may also be revealed in our body. For we who live are always delivered to death for Jesus’ sake, that the life also of Jesus may be revealed in our mortal flesh. So then death works in us, but life in you.
slang_text: we get hunted down but we don't get abandoned; we get knocked flat but we don't get wiped out. We're carryin' the Lord Jesus' death around in our own bodies everywhere we go, so His life can shine through our bodies too. Long as we're breathin', we're gettin' handed over to death for Jesus' sake, over and over, so His life shows up in our worn-out flesh. So death's doin' its thing in us — but that means life's doin' its thing in you.

web_text: But having the same spirit of faith, according to that which is written, “I believed, and therefore I spoke.” We also believe, and therefore also we speak; knowing that he who raised the Lord Jesus will raise us also with Jesus, and will present us with you. For all things are for your sakes, that the grace, being multiplied through the many, may cause the thanksgiving to abound to the glory of God. Therefore we don’t faint, but though our outward man is decaying, yet our inward man is renewed day by day.
slang_text: But we got that same spirit of faith goin', just like it says: "I believed, so I spoke up." We believe too, so we speak up too, knowin' that whoever raised the Lord Jesus is gonna raise us up right along with Jesus, and stand us up next to you. This is all for your sake, so that grace, spreadin' out to more and more people, can pile up thanksgiving to God's glory. So we don't chicken out — even though our outer shell's fallin' apart, our inside's gettin' made new every single day.

web_text: For our light affliction, which is for the moment, works for us more and more exceedingly an eternal weight of glory; while we don’t look at the things which are seen, but at the things which are not seen. For the things which are seen are temporal, but the things which are not seen are eternal.
slang_text: This little bit of grief we're catchin' right now don't even compare — it's cookin' up an eternal, epic weight of glory that just keeps stackin' up more and more. So we're not starin' at what we can see, we're lookin' at what we can't see. What you can see is here today, gone tomorrow; what you can't see is gonna last forever.

web_text: For we know that if the earthly house of our tent is dissolved, we have a building from God, a house not made with hands, eternal, in the heavens. For most certainly in this we groan, longing to be clothed with our habitation which is from heaven; if so be that being clothed we will not be found naked. For indeed we who are in this tent do groan, being burdened; not that we desire to be unclothed, but that we desire to be clothed, that what is mortal may be swallowed up by life.
slang_text: We know that if this earthly tent we're campin' out in gets torn down, we've got a building waitin' from God, a house nobody built with their own hands, eternal, up in the heavens. And man, do we groan in this old tent, achin' to get suited up in that heavenly home — 'cause once we're suited up, we won't be caught standin' there buck naked. Yeah, we groan under the weight of this old tent, not 'cause we wanna get stripped down, but 'cause we're dyin' to get suited up, so this mortal stuff gets swallowed whole by life.

web_text: Now he who made us for this very thing is God, who also gave to us the down payment of the Spirit. Therefore we are always confident and know that while we are at home in the body, we are absent from the Lord; for we walk by faith, not by sight. We are courageous, I say, and are willing rather to be absent from the body, and to be at home with the Lord.
slang_text: God's the one who cooked us up for exactly this, and He handed over the Spirit as a down payment on it. So we're always totally confident, knowin' that while we're chillin' at home in this body, we're actually away from the Lord — 'cause we walk by faith, not by what we can see. We're stayin' brave about it, and honestly we'd rather bounce outta this body and go home to the Lord.

web_text: Therefore also we make it our aim, whether at home or absent, to be well pleasing to him. For we must all be revealed before the judgment seat of Christ; that each one may receive the things in the body, according to what he has done, whether good or bad. Knowing therefore the fear of the Lord, we persuade men, but we are revealed to God; and I hope that we are revealed also in your consciences. For we are not commending ourselves to you again, but speak as giving you occasion of boasting on our behalf, that you may have something to answer those who boast in appearance, and not in heart.
slang_text: So whether we're home or away, our whole aim's just to make Him happy. 'Cause every last one of us has gotta stand exposed in front of Christ's judgment seat, gettin' back whatever we earned in this body, good or bogus. Knowin' how serious it is to fear the Lord, we work on convincin' folks, and God can see straight through us already — and I'm hopin' your consciences can see straight through us too. We're not braggin' on ourselves again here, just givin' you ammo to boast about us, so you got a comeback for the folks braggin' on looks instead of what's really in the heart.

web_text: For if we are beside ourselves, it is for God. Or if we are of sober mind, it is for you. For the love of Christ constrains us; because we judge thus, that one died for all, therefore all died. He died for all, that those who live should no longer live to themselves, but to him who for their sakes died and rose again. Therefore we know no one after the flesh from now on. Even though we have known Christ after the flesh, yet now we know him so no more.
slang_text: If we're actin' straight-up crazy, that's for God's sake; if we're keepin' it cool and sober, that's for you guys. 'Cause Christ's love's got us locked in, once we figured this out: one dude died for everybody, so everybody died with Him. He died for all so the ones still livin' wouldn't keep livin' for themselves anymore, but for the One who died and rose back up for their sake. So from here on out, we don't size nobody up by fleshly standards. Even if we used to size up Christ that way, we sure don't do it anymore.

web_text: Therefore if anyone is in Christ, he is a new creation. The old things have passed away. Behold, all things have become new. But all things are of God, who reconciled us to himself through Jesus Christ, and gave to us the ministry of reconciliation; namely, that God was in Christ reconciling the world to himself, not reckoning to them their trespasses, and having committed to us the word of reconciliation. We are therefore ambassadors on behalf of Christ, as though God were entreating by us: we beg you on behalf of Christ, be reconciled to God.
slang_text: So if anybody's in Christ, they're a brand-new creation, totally fresh. The old junk's outta here — check it out, everything's made new. And all of it comes from God, who patched things up between us and Him through Jesus Christ, and handed us the job of patchin' things up with everybody else too. God was right there in Christ, makin' peace with the whole world, not holdin' folks' screwups against 'em, and He put the word of peace-makin' in our hands. So we're basically ambassadors for Christ, like God's makin' His pitch straight through us — we're beggin' you, on Christ's behalf: make things right with God.

web_text: For him who knew no sin he made to be sin on our behalf; so that in him we might become the righteousness of God.
slang_text: God took the one guy who never sinned, not once, and made Him sin on our behalf, so that in Him we could become God's own righteousness. Way harsh, but that's grace for you.

web_text:
Working together, we entreat also that you do not receive the grace of God in vain, for he says,
“At an acceptable time I listened to you,
in a day of salvation I helped you.”
Behold, now is the acceptable time. Behold, now is the day of salvation. We give no occasion of stumbling in anything, that our service may not be blamed, but in everything commending ourselves, as servants of God, in great endurance, in afflictions, in hardships, in distresses,
slang_text: Workin' side by side with Him, we're beggin' you too — don't let God's grace go to waste. He said, "At the right time, I listened to you; on the day of salvation, I helped you out." Check it out, right now's the right time. Right now's the day of salvation. We don't put stumblin' blocks in nobody's path, so our ministry can't get bad-mouthed, but we back ourselves up in everything, like real servants of God oughta — stayin' tough through it all, through afflictions, hardships, rough patches,

web_text: in beatings, in imprisonments, in riots, in labors, in watchings, in fastings; in pureness, in knowledge, in perseverance, in kindness, in the Holy Spirit, in sincere love, in the word of truth, in the power of God; by the armor of righteousness on the right hand and on the left, by glory and dishonor, by evil report and good report; as deceivers, and yet true;
slang_text: through beatdowns, lockups, riots, backbreakin' work, sleepless nights, and fasts; through stayin' pure, stayin' sharp with knowledge, stayin' patient, stayin' kind, through the Holy Spirit, through love that's the real deal, through the truth, through God's own power; suited up in righteousness like armor, left hand and right; whether we're gettin' props or gettin' dissed, whether folks are talkin' trash or singin' our praises; called liars, but we're the real deal;

web_text: as unknown, and yet well known; as dying, and behold, we live; as punished, and not killed; as sorrowful, yet always rejoicing; as poor, yet making many rich; as having nothing, and yet possessing all things. Our mouth is open to you, Corinthians. Our heart is enlarged. You are not restricted by us, but you are restricted by your own affections.
slang_text: treated like nobodies, yet everybody knows us; like we're dyin', and check it, we're alive and kickin'; punished, but not wiped out; bummed out, but always stoked underneath it; broke, but makin' a ton of folks rich; owning nothin', yet ownin' it all. Our mouths are wide open to you, Corinthians. Our hearts are cracked wide open too. We're not the ones holdin' back on you — you're the ones holdin' back on your own feelings.

web_text: Now in return, I speak as to my children, you also open your hearts. Don’t be unequally yoked with unbelievers, for what fellowship have righteousness and iniquity? Or what fellowship has light with darkness? What agreement has Christ with Belial? Or what portion has a believer with an unbeliever? What agreement has a temple of God with idols? For you are a temple of the living God. Even as God said, “I will dwell in them, and walk in them; and I will be their God, and they will be my people.”
slang_text: So do the same back, I'm talkin' to you like my own kids — crack your hearts open too. Don't go teamin' up with unbelievers, it's a bogus mismatch — what's righteousness got to do with sin, anyway? What's light got in common with darkness? What deal's Christ got with the devil? What's a believer got in common with somebody who's not? What's God's temple got to do with idols? 'Cause you ARE the temple of the livin' God. Like God Himself said: "I'm gonna move right in and live among 'em, and walk right alongside 'em; I'll be their God, and they'll be my people."

web_text:
Therefore
“‘Come out from among them,
and be separate,’ says the Lord.
‘Touch no unclean thing.
I will receive you. I will be to you a Father.
You will be to me sons and daughters,’
says the Lord Almighty.”
slang_text: So, like the Lord says: "Get out from among 'em, and stay separate. Don't touch nothin' grody or unclean. I'll take you in. I'll be your Father, and you'll be my sons and daughters" — straight from the Lord Almighty.

web_text: Having therefore these promises, beloved, let us cleanse ourselves from all defilement of flesh and spirit, perfecting holiness in the fear of God. Open your hearts to us. We wronged no one. We corrupted no one. We took advantage of no one. I say this not to condemn you, for I have said before, that you are in our hearts to die together and live together. Great is my boldness of speech toward you. Great is my boasting on your behalf. I am filled with comfort. I overflow with joy in all our affliction.
slang_text: So, seein' as we got promises like these, dudes, let's scrub ourselves clean of every bit of grody stuff, flesh and spirit both, and go all the way with holiness, fearin' God the whole way. Crack your hearts open to us. We never wronged nobody. We never messed nobody up. We never took advantage of a single soul. I'm not sayin' this to bust your chops — I already told you, you're locked in our hearts, dyin' together and livin' together. I'm talkin' to you bold, no holdin' back. I'm braggin' on you big time. I'm totally loaded up with comfort. I'm overflowin' with joy even in all our grief.

web_text: For even when we had come into Macedonia, our flesh had no relief, but we were afflicted on every side. Fightings were outside. Fear was inside. Nevertheless, he who comforts the lowly, God, comforted us by the coming of Titus; and not by his coming only, but also by the comfort with which he was comforted in you, while he told us of your longing, your mourning, and your zeal for me; so that I rejoiced still more. For though I made you sorry with my letter, I do not regret it, though I did regret it. For I see that my letter made you sorry, though just for a while.
slang_text: Even after we rolled into Macedonia, we couldn't catch a break — we were catchin' heat from every angle. Fights outside, fear inside. But God, who comforts the down-and-out, comforted us when Titus showed up — and not just him showin' up, but the comfort he'd gotten from you guys, tellin' us how much you missed us, how bummed you were, how fired up you were for me, so I was even more stoked. Even though my letter bummed you out, I'm not sorry about it — well, I was sorry for a hot minute, but I can see now it only bummed you out for a little while.

web_text: I now rejoice, not that you were made sorry, but that you were made sorry to repentance. For you were made sorry in a godly way, that you might suffer loss by us in nothing. For godly sorrow produces repentance to salvation, which brings no regret. But the sorrow of the world produces death. For behold, this same thing, that you were made sorry in a godly way, what earnest care it worked in you. Yes, what defense, indignation, fear, longing, zeal, and vengeance! In everything you demonstrated yourselves to be pure in the matter. So although I wrote to you, I wrote not for his cause that did the wrong, nor for his cause that suffered the wrong, but that your earnest care for us might be revealed in you in the sight of God.
slang_text: Now I'm stoked — not 'cause you got bummed out, but 'cause that bummer turned into real repentance. You got sorry the godly way, so we never cost you nothin' in the end. Godly sorrow works up repentance that leads to salvation, no regrets attached. But the world's kinda sorrow just cooks up death. Check it out — this same godly sorrow, look how much it fired you up to care! Standin' up for yourselves, gettin' indignant, gettin' scared, missin' us, gettin' zealous, wantin' justice done! You proved yourselves totally clean in the whole deal, top to bottom. So even though I wrote you, it wasn't about the guy who did wrong or the guy who got wronged — it was so your care for us would come out plain, right in front of God.

web_text: Therefore we have been comforted. In our comfort we rejoiced the more exceedingly for the joy of Titus, because his spirit has been refreshed by you all. For if in anything I have boasted to him on your behalf, I was not disappointed. But as we spoke all things to you in truth, so our glorying also which I made before Titus was found to be truth. His affection is more abundantly toward you, while he remembers all of your obedience, how with fear and trembling you received him. I rejoice that in everything I am confident concerning you.
slang_text: That's why we're comforted. And on top of our own comfort, we were even more stoked over Titus bein' stoked, 'cause you guys refreshed his spirit big time. Whatever I bragged to him about you, I wasn't left lookin' bogus. Just like everything we told you was straight-up true, so was our braggin' to Titus — it all checked out. His love for you runs even deeper now, rememberin' how you all obeyed, how you welcomed him with fear and tremblin'. I'm stoked that I can trust you all the way through, no doubts.

web_text: Moreover, brothers, we make known to you the grace of God which has been given in the assemblies of Macedonia; how that in much proof of affliction the abundance of their joy and their deep poverty abounded to the riches of their generosity. For according to their power, I testify, yes and beyond their power, they gave of their own accord, begging us with much entreaty to receive this grace and the fellowship in the service to the saints.
slang_text: Now, brothers, check out what we gotta tell you about God's grace showin' up in the churches over in Macedonia. Even goin' through some seriously rough patches, their joy went off the charts and their dirt-poor situation somehow turned into off-the-charts generosity. I'll vouch for it — they gave everything they had, and then some, totally on their own steam, practically beggin' us to let 'em in on this grace, this chance to pitch in for the saints.

web_text: This was not as we had expected, but first they gave their own selves to the Lord, and to us through the will of God. So we urged Titus, that as he had made a beginning before, so he would also complete in you this grace. But as you abound in everything, in faith, utterance, knowledge, all earnestness, and in your love to us, see that you also abound in this grace. I speak not by way of commandment, but as proving through the earnestness of others the sincerity also of your love.
slang_text: That wasn't even what we saw comin' — first off, they gave themselves to the Lord, and to us too, all 'cause that's what God wanted. So we pushed Titus, since he already got this thing started with you guys, to go finish the job. And since you're already loaded up in everything — faith, speakin' up, knowledge, all that hustle, plus your love for us — make sure you're loaded up in this generosity thing too. I'm not layin' down orders here, just usin' other folks' hustle to test how real your love is.

web_text: For you know the grace of our Lord Jesus Christ, that, though he was rich, yet for your sakes he became poor, that you through his poverty might become rich. I give a judgment in this: for this is expedient for you, who were the first to start a year ago, not only to do, but also to be willing. But now complete the doing also, that as there was the readiness to be willing, so there may be the completion also out of your ability. For if the readiness is there, it is acceptable according to what you have, not according to what you don’t have.
slang_text: 'Cause you already know the grace of our Lord Jesus Christ — how He was loaded, but went dirt-poor for your sake, so through His poverty you could get rich. Here's my two cents: it's a good look for you, since you were the first ones to jump on this a year back, not just wantin' to do it but actually bein' down for it. So now go ahead and finish the job — let your ability match the eagerness you already showed. 'Cause if you're willin', God's cool with whatever you've got, not what you don't got.

web_text: For this is not that others may be eased and you distressed, but for equality. Your abundance at this present time supplies their lack, that their abundance also may become a supply for your lack; that there may be equality. As it is written, “He who gathered much had nothing left over, and he who gathered little had no lack.” But thanks be to God, who puts the same earnest care for you into the heart of Titus.
slang_text: This isn't about makin' everybody else comfy while you get squeezed — it's about keepin' things even. What you've got extra right now covers what they're missin', so what they've got extra later can cover what you're missin' — keepin' it even all around. Like it's written: "The dude who gathered a ton had nothin' left over, and the dude who gathered a little came up short of nothin'." But thanks be to God, who put that same fired-up care for you right into Titus' heart.

web_text: For he indeed accepted our exhortation, but being himself very earnest, he went out to you of his own accord. We have sent together with him the brother whose praise in the Good News is known throughout all the assemblies. Not only so, but he was also appointed by the assemblies to travel with us in this grace, which is served by us to the glory of the Lord himself, and to show our readiness. We are avoiding this, that any man should blame us concerning this abundance which is administered by us.
slang_text: He took our advice, sure, but he was already so fired up he headed your way on his own steam anyway. We're sendin' along with him a brother whose rep for the Good News is known in every church out there. Not just that — the churches also picked him to travel with us on this generosity gig, which we're runnin' for the Lord's own glory and to show how ready we are. We're steerin' clear of any way somebody could bad-mouth us over how we handle all this cash.

web_text: Having regard for honorable things, not only in the sight of the Lord, but also in the sight of men. We have sent with them our brother, whom we have many times proved earnest in many things, but now much more earnest, by reason of the great confidence which he has in you. As for Titus, he is my partner and fellow worker for you. As for our brothers, they are the apostles of the assemblies, the glory of Christ. Therefore show the proof of your love to them before the assemblies, and of our boasting on your behalf.
slang_text: We're keepin' it honorable, not just in front of the Lord but in front of everybody watchin' too. We're sendin' along with 'em another brother we've tested plenty of times and found fired-up in tons of stuff, and now he's even more fired up 'cause he's so confident about you guys. As for Titus, he's my partner, workin' alongside me for you. As for our brothers, they're the churches' own messengers, straight-up Christ's glory in the flesh. So show 'em the real deal of your love, right out where the churches can see it, and back up what we've been braggin' about you.

web_text: It is indeed unnecessary for me to write to you concerning the service to the saints, for I know your readiness, of which I boast on your behalf to them of Macedonia, that Achaia has been prepared for a year past. Your zeal has stirred up very many of them. But I have sent the brothers that our boasting on your behalf may not be in vain in this respect, that, just as I said, you may be prepared, lest by any means, if anyone from Macedonia comes there with me and finds you unprepared, we (to say nothing of you) would be disappointed in this confident boasting.
slang_text: I don't even really gotta write you about this whole servin'-the-saints thing, 'cause I already know you're ready — I've been braggin' about it to the Macedonia crowd, tellin' 'em Achaia's had it together for a whole year now. Your hustle's fired up a ton of 'em. But I'm sendin' the brothers anyway, so my braggin' on you doesn't turn out bogus, so you're actually ready like I said — 'cause if some Macedonians roll in with me and find you unprepared, we'd look like total flakes on this confident braggin' (not to mention how it'd land on you).

web_text: I thought it necessary therefore to entreat the brothers that they would go before to you, and arrange ahead of time the generous gift that you promised before, that the same might be ready as a matter of generosity, and not of greediness. Remember this: he who sows sparingly will also reap sparingly. He who sows bountifully will also reap bountifully. Let each man give according as he has determined in his heart; not grudgingly, or under compulsion; for God loves a cheerful giver. And God is able to make all grace abound to you, that you, always having all sufficiency in everything, may abound to every good work.
slang_text: So I figured I better ask the brothers to head over ahead of us and get that generous gift you already promised squared away in advance, so it's ready as somethin' you're really givin', not somethin' you're squeezin' out like it's a shakedown. Remember this: whoever plants stingy is gonna harvest stingy. Whoever plants generous is gonna harvest generous. Everybody's gotta give whatever they decided in their own heart, not grudgin' about it or feelin' forced — God's into a cheerful giver, totally. And God can pile up all kinds of grace on you, so you've always got what you need for everything, plus plenty left over for every good deed out there.

Just like it's written: "He scattered his stuff far and wide, he gave to the poor. His righteousness sticks around forever." Now may the one who hands out seed to the sower and bread to eat, hand out and multiply your seed for plantin', and pile up the fruit of your righteousness even more — makin' you rich in everything so you can be generous across the board, which turns right around into thanksgiving to God through us. 'Cause this generosity gig you're runnin' doesn't just cover what the saints are missin', it also stacks up a mountain of thanks to God;

web_text: seeing that through the proof given by this service, they glorify God for the obedience of your confession to the Good News of Christ, and for the generosity of your contribution to them and to all; while they themselves also, with supplication on your behalf, yearn for you by reason of the exceeding grace of God in you. Now thanks be to God for his unspeakable gift!
slang_text: since through this whole generosity thing provin' itself out, they're gonna praise God for how you stayed obedient to your confession of the Good News about Christ, and for how generous you were sharin' with them and everybody else. And they'll be prayin' for you too, achin' to meet you, on account of the crazy-abundant grace of God workin' in you. Thanks be to God for a gift that's straight-up beyond words!

web_text: Now I Paul, myself, entreat you by the humility and gentleness of Christ; I who in your presence am lowly among you, but being absent am bold toward you. Yes, I beg you that I may not, when present, show courage with the confidence with which I intend to be bold against some, who consider us to be walking according to the flesh. For though we walk in the flesh, we don’t wage war according to the flesh; for the weapons of our warfare are not of the flesh, but mighty before God to the throwing down of strongholds,
slang_text: Now it's me, Paul, personally beggin' you by the humility and gentleness of Christ — the same guy who acts all lowly to your face but talks bold from a distance, or so folks say. Well, I'm hopin' I don't gotta get bold face-to-face, with the same confidence I'm plannin' to use against the ones who figure we're just operatin' the fleshly way. 'Cause even though we're walkin' around in these bodies, we're not wagin' war the fleshly way — our weapons aren't outta this world, they're straight-up mighty in God's eyes, tough enough to knock down whole fortresses,

web_text: throwing down imaginations and every high thing that is exalted against the knowledge of God, and bringing every thought into captivity to the obedience of Christ; and being in readiness to avenge all disobedience, when your obedience will be made full. Do you look at things only as they appear in front of your face? If anyone trusts in himself that he is Christ’s, let him consider this again with himself, that, even as he is Christ’s, so also we are Christ’s. For though I should boast somewhat abundantly concerning our authority, (which the Lord gave for building you up, and not for casting you down) I will not be disappointed,
slang_text: knockin' down bogus arguments and every uppity notion that puffs itself up against knowin' God, and takin' every single thought captive to get it obedient to Christ; and we're standin' ready to smack down any disobedience, once you guys are all-the-way obedient. Are you just judgin' stuff by what's starin' you in the face? If some dude's convinced he's Christ's, let him think it over again — same as he belongs to Christ, so do we. 'Cause even if I got to braggin' a bit much about our authority (which the Lord gave us to build you up, not tear you down), I wouldn't come out lookin' bogus,

web_text: that I may not seem as if I desire to terrify you by my letters. For, “His letters”, they say, “are weighty and strong, but his bodily presence is weak, and his speech is despised.” Let such a person consider this, that what we are in word by letters when we are absent, such are we also in deed when we are present. For we are not bold to number or compare ourselves with some of those who commend themselves. But they themselves, measuring themselves by themselves, and comparing themselves with themselves, are without understanding.
slang_text: so it doesn't look like I'm just tryin' to scare you outta your minds with my letters. 'Cause folks say, "His letters pack a punch, heavy and tough, but face-to-face he's a total wimp, and his speakin' is nothin' to write home about." Whoever's sayin' that oughta think it over: whatever we sound like on paper from far away, that's exactly what we'll be in person when we show up. We're not brave enough to rank ourselves or size ourselves up against the folks who go around braggin' on themselves. But those clowns are measurin' themselves against themselves, comparin' themselves to themselves — that's just clueless.

web_text: But we will not boast beyond proper limits, but within the boundaries with which God appointed to us, which reach even to you. For we don’t stretch ourselves too much, as though we didn’t reach to you. For we came even as far as to you with the Good News of Christ, not boasting beyond proper limits in other men’s labors, but having hope that as your faith grows, we will be abundantly enlarged by you in our sphere of influence, so as to preach the Good News even to the parts beyond you, not to boast in what someone else has already done.
slang_text: But we're not gonna brag past the lines God actually drew for us, and those lines stretch all the way to you. We're not overreachin' here, like we never even made it to you. We came all the way to you with the Good News about Christ — that's a fact. We're not braggin' past our limits over somebody else's hard work; we're just hopin' that as your faith grows, our turf's gonna grow right along with you, so we can spread the Good News even further out past you, without braggin' on stuff some other guy already got done.

web_text: But “he who boasts, let him boast in the Lord.” For it isn’t he who commends himself who is approved, but whom the Lord commends.
slang_text: But like it says, "whoever's braggin', let him brag on the Lord." 'Cause it's not the guy who sings his own praises who passes the test — it's the guy the Lord vouches for.

web_text: I wish that you would bear with me in a little foolishness, but indeed you do bear with me. For I am jealous over you with a godly jealousy. For I married you to one husband, that I might present you as a pure virgin to Christ. But I am afraid that somehow, as the serpent deceived Eve in his craftiness, so your minds might be corrupted from the simplicity that is in Christ. For if he who comes preaches another Jesus, whom we did not preach, or if you receive a different spirit, which you did not receive, or a different “good news”, which you did not accept, you put up with that well enough.
slang_text: I wish you'd put up with a little bit of foolishness from me here — and hey, you already do put up with me, so cool. I'm jealous over you with a godly kinda jealousy. I hooked you up with one husband, so I could hand you over pure and spotless to Christ, like a virgin bride. But I'm scared that somehow, just like that snake conned Eve with his slick moves, your minds might get corrupted away from the simple, straight-up thing you've got goin' with Christ. 'Cause if some dude rolls in preachin' a different Jesus than the one we preached, or you get handed a different spirit than the one you already got, or a different "good news" than the one you already bought into — you put up with that just fine, no problem.

web_text: For I reckon that I am not at all behind the very best apostles. But though I am unskilled in speech, yet I am not unskilled in knowledge. No, in every way we have been revealed to you in all things. Or did I commit a sin in humbling myself that you might be exalted, because I preached to you God’s Good News free of charge? I robbed other assemblies, taking wages from them that I might serve you.
slang_text: 'Cause I figure I'm not one bit behind even the top-shelf apostles out there. Maybe I'm no smooth talker, but I'm sure not clueless when it comes to knowledge. No way — we've laid it all out plain for you, every which way. Or did I mess up by makin' myself lowly so you could get lifted up, seein' as I preached God's Good News to you for free? I basically ripped off other churches, takin' their cash so I could serve you guys for nothin'.

web_text: When I was present with you and was in need, I wasn’t a burden on anyone, for the brothers, when they came from Macedonia, supplied the measure of my need. In everything I kept myself from being burdensome to you, and I will continue to do so. As the truth of Christ is in me, no one will stop me from this boasting in the regions of Achaia. Why? Because I don’t love you? God knows. But what I do, that I will do, that I may cut off occasion from them that desire an occasion, that in which they boast, they may be found even as we.
slang_text: When I was with you guys and comin' up short, I never leaned on nobody, 'cause the brothers who came down from Macedonia covered what I needed. I kept myself from bein' a burden to you in every way, and I'm gonna keep doin' it. Long as the truth of Christ is in me, ain't nobody gonna shut down this bit of braggin' I'm doin' all through Achaia. Why? 'Cause I don't love you? God knows I do. But what I'm doin', I'm gonna keep doin', so I cut off any excuse for the guys lookin' for an excuse to say they're doin' the exact same thing as us.

web_text: For such men are false apostles, deceitful workers, masquerading as Christ’s apostles. And no wonder, for even Satan masquerades as an angel of light. It is no great thing therefore if his servants also masquerade as servants of righteousness, whose end will be according to their works. I say again, let no one think me foolish. But if so, yet receive me as foolish, that I also may boast a little.
slang_text: 'Cause guys like that are bogus apostles, straight-up con artists dressed up like they work for Christ. And that's no shocker, 'cause even Satan dresses himself up like an angel of light. So it's no big deal if his flunkies dress up like servants of righteousness too — they're gonna get exactly what their bogus work's got comin'. I'll say it again: don't nobody go thinkin' I'm actin' the fool. But if you do, then just go with it — let me act the fool a little, so I can do some braggin' of my own.

web_text: That which I speak, I don’t speak according to the Lord, but as in foolishness, in this confidence of boasting. Seeing that many boast after the flesh, I will also boast. For you bear with the foolish gladly, being wise. For you bear with a man, if he brings you into bondage, if he devours you, if he takes you captive, if he exalts himself, if he strikes you on the face.
slang_text: What I'm about to say, I'm not sayin' it like the Lord would say it — I'm sayin' it like a fool, all confident in this braggin' game. Since a ton of guys brag the fleshly way, I'm gonna brag too. You're so wise, you put up with fools gladly, no problem. Shoot, you'll put up with a dude if he chains you up, if he eats you alive, if he takes you prisoner, if he puffs himself up, if he flat-out slaps you across the face.

web_text: I speak by way of disparagement, as though we had been weak. Yet in whatever way anyone is bold (I speak in foolishness), I am bold also. Are they Hebrews? So am I. Are they Israelites? So am I. Are they the offspring of Abraham? So am I. Are they servants of Christ? (I speak as one beside himself) I am more so; in labors more abundantly, in prisons more abundantly, in stripes above measure, in deaths often. Five times from the Jews I received forty stripes minus one.
slang_text: I'm sayin' this to shame us, like we were too weak to pull that stuff. But whatever anybody else is bold enough to claim (I'm talkin' foolish here), I'm bold enough to claim it too. They Hebrews? So am I. They Israelites? So am I. They Abraham's own bloodline? So am I. They servants of Christ? (Now I'm really talkin' crazy) I'm more of one than they are — way more labor, way more lockups, beaten half to death more times than I can count, starin' down death over and over. Five separate times the Jews gave me forty lashes minus one.

web_text: Three times I was beaten with rods. Once I was stoned. Three times I suffered shipwreck. I have been a night and a day in the deep. I have been in travels often, perils of rivers, perils of robbers, perils from my countrymen, perils from the Gentiles, perils in the city, perils in the wilderness, perils in the sea, perils among false brothers; in labor and travail, in watchings often, in hunger and thirst, in fastings often, and in cold and nakedness. Besides those things that are outside, there is that which presses on me daily: anxiety for all the assemblies.
slang_text: Three times they beat me with rods. Once they flat-out stoned me. Three times I got shipwrecked. I spent a whole day and night floatin' in the open water. I've been on the road nonstop, dodgin' danger from rivers, danger from bandits, danger from my own people, danger from the Gentiles, danger in the city, danger out in the wilderness, danger at sea, danger from fake brothers pretendin' to be on our side; through backbreakin' work, sleepless nights, hunger and thirst, fasts on top of fasts, freezin' cold with barely a shirt on my back. And on top of all that outside stuff, there's the thing that weighs on me every single day: worryin' about every church out there.

web_text: Who is weak, and I am not weak? Who is caused to stumble, and I don’t burn with indignation? If I must boast, I will boast of the things that concern my weakness. The God and Father of the Lord Jesus Christ, he who is blessed forever more, knows that I don’t lie. In Damascus the governor under King Aretas guarded the Damascenes’ city, desiring to arrest me.
slang_text: Who's weak, that I don't feel weak right along with 'em? Who gets tripped up, that I don't burn with anger over it? If I gotta brag, I'm gonna brag about my own weak spots. The God and Father of the Lord Jesus Christ, blessed forever, knows I'm not lyin' about any of this. Back in Damascus, the governor under King Aretas had the whole city locked down, tryin' to nab me.

web_text: Through a window I was let down in a basket by the wall, and escaped his hands.
slang_text: But they lowered me out a window in a basket, over the city wall, and I slipped right outta his hands. Real smooth escape, if I do say so myself.

web_text: It is doubtless not profitable for me to boast. For I will come to visions and revelations of the Lord. I know a man in Christ, fourteen years ago (whether in the body, I don’t know, or whether out of the body, I don’t know; God knows), such a one caught up into the third heaven. I know such a man (whether in the body, or outside of the body, I don’t know; God knows), how he was caught up into Paradise, and heard unspeakable words, which it is not lawful for a man to utter.
slang_text: Braggin' probably won't get me nowhere, but I'll go there anyway — let's talk visions and revelations from the Lord. I know a guy in Christ, fourteen years back now (whether he was in the body or outta the body, I got no clue, God knows), who got snatched clean up to the third heaven. I know this guy (in the body or outta the body, still no clue, God knows) got hauled up into Paradise and heard stuff so wild there's no words for it, stuff no human's even allowed to repeat.

web_text: On behalf of such a one I will boast, but on my own behalf I will not boast, except in my weaknesses. For if I would desire to boast, I will not be foolish; for I will speak the truth. But I refrain, so that no man may think more of me than that which he sees in me, or hears from me. By reason of the exceeding greatness of the revelations, that I should not be exalted excessively, there was given to me a thorn in the flesh, a messenger of Satan to torment me, that I should not be exalted excessively. Concerning this thing, I begged the Lord three times that it might depart from me.
slang_text: I'll brag on behalf of a guy like that, but for myself, I'm not braggin' on nothin' except my own weak spots. If I wanted to brag, I still wouldn't be actin' the fool, 'cause I'd be tellin' the truth. But I'm holdin' back, so nobody thinks more of me than what they can actually see in me or hear outta me. And 'cause these revelations were so over-the-top epic, so I wouldn't get too big for my britches, I got handed a thorn in my flesh, a messenger straight from Satan to torment me, keepin' me from gettin' puffed up. I begged the Lord three separate times to yank that thing outta me.

web_text:
He has said to me,
“My grace is sufficient for you, for my power is made perfect in weakness.” Most gladly therefore I will rather glory in my weaknesses, that the power of Christ may rest on me. Therefore I take pleasure in weaknesses, in injuries, in necessities, in persecutions, in distresses, for Christ’s sake. For when I am weak, then am I strong. I have become foolish in boasting. You compelled me, for I ought to have been commended by you, for in nothing was I inferior to the very best apostles, though I am nothing. Truly the signs of an apostle were worked among you in all perseverance, in signs and wonders and mighty works.
slang_text: But He told me straight up: "My grace is all you need — my power hits its peak when you're weak." So I'll gladly brag about my weak spots instead, so Christ's power can set up shop right on top of me. That's why I'm cool with weaknesses, injuries, hard times, gettin' persecuted, rough patches, all for Christ's sake. 'Cause when I'm weak, that's exactly when I'm strong. I've gone full-on fool with all this braggin' — but you made me do it, since you're the ones who oughta be vouchin' for me. I wasn't behind the top-shelf apostles in nothin', even though I'm nothin' myself. The real signs of an apostle got worked out right in front of you, patiently, through signs and wonders and mighty deeds.

web_text: For what is there in which you were made inferior to the rest of the assemblies, unless it is that I myself was not a burden to you? Forgive me this wrong. Behold, this is the third time I am ready to come to you, and I will not be a burden to you; for I seek not your possessions, but you. For the children ought not to save up for the parents, but the parents for the children. I will most gladly spend and be spent for your souls. If I love you more abundantly, am I loved the less? But be it so, I did not myself burden you. But, being crafty, I caught you with deception.
slang_text: What'd you guys get shorted on, compared to the other churches, except that I never leaned on you for cash? Forgive me for that bogus wrong I did you. Check it out, this is the third time I'm ready to head your way, and I still won't be a burden — I'm not after your stuff, I'm after you. Kids shouldn't be savin' up for their parents, it's the parents savin' up for the kids. I'll gladly burn myself out, spend everything I got, for your souls. If I love you this hard, does that mean you love me less? Fine, so I never burdened you myself — but maybe I'm just sneaky and conned you some other way, huh?

web_text: Did I take advantage of you by anyone of them whom I have sent to you? I exhorted Titus, and I sent the brother with him. Did Titus take any advantage of you? Didn’t we walk in the same spirit? Didn’t we walk in the same steps? Again, do you think that we are excusing ourselves to you? In the sight of God we speak in Christ. But all things, beloved, are for your edifying. For I am afraid that by any means, when I come, I might find you not the way I want to, and that I might be found by you as you don’t desire; that by any means there would be strife, jealousy, outbursts of anger, factions, slander, whisperings, proud thoughts, riots;
slang_text: Did I take advantage of you through anybody I sent your way? I pushed Titus to go, and sent the brother along with him. Did Titus take advantage of you at all? Didn't we walk in the exact same spirit? Same steps, even? You think we're just makin' excuses to you again? We're speakin' in Christ, right in front of God. But everything we're sayin', dudes, is for buildin' you up. 'Cause I'm scared that when I show up, I might find you not actin' how I want, and you might find me not actin' how you want — that there'd be fightin', jealousy, blowups of anger, cliques, trash-talkin', gossip, puffed-up egos, chaos everywhere;

web_text: that again when I come my God would humble me before you, and I would mourn for many of those who have sinned before now, and not repented of the uncleanness and sexual immorality and lustfulness which they committed.
slang_text: and that when I show up again, my God would humble me right in front of you, and I'd end up grievin' over a ton of folks who sinned before and never repented of all the grody stuff — the sexual immorality and lust they got tangled up in.

web_text: This is the third time I am coming to you. “At the mouth of two or three witnesses shall every word be established.” I have said beforehand, and I do say beforehand, as when I was present the second time, so now, being absent, I write to those who have sinned before now, and to all the rest, that, if I come again, I will not spare; seeing that you seek a proof of Christ who speaks in me; who toward you is not weak, but is powerful in you. For he was crucified through weakness, yet he lives through the power of God. For we also are weak in him, but we will live with him through the power of God toward you.
slang_text: This makes the third time I'm headin' your way. "Every charge gets nailed down by two or three witnesses." I already said it once, and I'm sayin' it again — same as when I was there the second time, I'm sayin' it now from a distance too: to the folks who sinned before, and to everybody else, if I come again, I'm not goin' easy on nobody. Seein' as you're lookin' for proof that Christ is really talkin' through me — He's not weak toward you, He's straight-up powerful in you. He got crucified outta weakness, sure, but He's alive now through God's power. We're weak in Him too, but we're gonna live right alongside Him, through God's power, workin' for you.

web_text: Examine your own selves, whether you are in the faith. Test your own selves. Or don’t you know as to your own selves, that Jesus Christ is in you?—unless indeed you are disqualified. But I hope that you will know that we aren’t disqualified. Now I pray to God that you do no evil; not that we may appear approved, but that you may do that which is honorable, though we are as reprobate. For we can do nothing against the truth, but for the truth.
slang_text: Check yourselves out, see if you're really in the faith. Put yourselves to the test. Or don't you already know Jesus Christ is livin' in you — unless you've flunked the test somehow? But I'm hopin' you'll figure out we haven't flunked it. Now I'm prayin' to God you don't do nothin' evil — not so we look good passin' the test, but so you do what's honorable, even if we end up lookin' like we flunked. 'Cause we can't do a thing against the truth, only for it.

web_text: For we rejoice when we are weak and you are strong. And this we also pray for, even your perfecting. For this cause I write these things while absent, that I may not deal sharply when present, according to the authority which the Lord gave me for building up, and not for tearing down. Finally, brothers, rejoice. Be perfected, be comforted, be of the same mind, live in peace, and the God of love and peace will be with you. Greet one another with a holy kiss.
slang_text: We're stoked when we're weak and you guys are strong. That's what we're prayin' for too — that you get made complete. That's why I'm writin' all this from a distance, so I don't gotta come down hard on you in person, usin' the authority the Lord gave me for buildin' up, not tearin' down. So finally, brothers, be stoked. Get it together, get comforted, stay on the same page, live in peace, and the God of love and peace is gonna be right there with you. Greet each other with a holy kiss.

web_text: All the saints greet you. The grace of the Lord Jesus Christ, God’s love, and the fellowship of the Holy Spirit, be with you all. Amen.
slang_text: All the saints out here send their love. The grace of the Lord Jesus Christ, God's love, and hangin' tight with the Holy Spirit — that's what I'm wishin' for every last one of you. Amen.
